name: Fredes Pass Loop from Rosell
name_original: Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà — Tour desde Rosell hasta el puerto de Fredes
description: This difficult 39.2-mile road cycling route from Rosell to the Port of Fredes offers stunning views and challenging climbs in the Tinença de
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Rossell, located in Castellón, Spain, traverse a landscape characterized by its rural terrain and proximity to the Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park. The region features varied topography, including significant climbs and descents, with natural features such as gorges, rivers, and the Ulldecona Reservoir. Road cyclists can expect routes that navigate through traditional agricultural areas and offer views of the surrounding natural environment. Best road cycling routes around Rossell The most popular road cycling route is Fredes Pass Loop from Rosell, a 39.2 miles (63.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 26 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain within the Tinença de Benifassà massif. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Sènia - Alcanar - Ermita del Remei - Castillo de Ulldecona – return through the Montsià, a moderate 43.7 miles (70.4 km) path. It offers a diverse ride through coastal and inland areas, passing historical sites. Local road cyclists also love the Ulldecona Reservoir – Vallibona loop from la Sénia, a 69.4 miles (111.7 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and past the Ulldecona Reservoir, often completed in about 6 hours 14 minutes. Road cycling around Rossell is defined by challenging climbs, rural roads, and views of natural parks and reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Rossell are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 1,300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Rossell's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Rossell?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes around Rossell, offering a variety of experiences. The region features routes ranging from moderate to difficult, with 5 easy, 42 moderate, and 11 difficult options to choose from.

Q: What is the typical terrain and difficulty for road cycling in Rossell?
A: Road cycling in Rossell, located in Castellón, Spain, is characterized by varied topography, including significant climbs and descents. Routes often navigate through rural terrain and the Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park, offering challenging and scenic rides. You can expect a mix of rural roads and mountainous sections.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Rossell?
A: Given its location in Castellón, Spain, Rossell generally offers favorable conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide the most comfortable temperatures for tackling the region's challenging climbs and enjoying the scenic views without extreme heat or cold. Summers can be warm, while winters are generally mild but may have cooler temperatures at higher elevations.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced road cyclists in Rossell?
A: While Rossell is known for its challenging routes, there are 5 routes classified as easy and 42 as moderate. These options can provide a good starting point for less experienced riders to enjoy the region's beauty without overly demanding climbs. It's always recommended to check the route profile before setting out.

Q: What are some of the more challenging road cycling routes in the Rossell area?
A: For experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e882122226/fredes-pass-loop-from-rosell" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fredes Pass Loop from Rosell&lt;/a&gt; stand out. This 39.2-mile (63.1 km) trail features significant elevation gain within the Tinença de Benifassà massif. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31509660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir – Vallibona loop from la Sénia&lt;/a&gt;, a 69.4-mile (111.7 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with substantial climbing.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Rossell?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Rossell are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e882122226/fredes-pass-loop-from-rosell" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fredes Pass Loop from Rosell&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31509660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir – Vallibona loop from la Sénia&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for exploring the region.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see along the road cycling routes in Rossell?
A: Road cycling routes in Rossell offer stunning natural scenery. You can expect to see the diverse landscapes of the Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park, including gorges and rivers. Notable natural attractions include the magnificent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3425404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; with its varying water colors, and the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3865075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel and Fountain of Sant Vicent&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Rossell?
A: The road cycling experience in Rossell is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, the beauty of the rural roads, and the stunning views of natural parks and reservoirs. The diverse terrain and well-curated routes are frequently highlighted as key attractions.

Q: Are there any routes that combine coastal and inland scenery?
A: Yes, for a diverse ride that combines different landscapes, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966570356/la-senia-alcanar-ermita-del-remei-castillo-de-ulldecona-return-through-the-montsia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sènia - Alcanar - Ermita del Remei - Castillo de Ulldecona – return through the Montsià&lt;/a&gt; route. This moderate 43.7-mile (70.4 km) path offers a varied experience through both coastal and inland areas, passing by historical sites along the way.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through traditional villages or settlements?
A: Many routes in the Rossell area traverse traditional agricultural areas and rural landscapes, offering glimpses into local life. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31509660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir – Vallibona loop from la Sénia&lt;/a&gt; passes near the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5527451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallibona&lt;/a&gt;, allowing cyclists to experience the region's cultural heritage.

Q: Are there any routes that include significant historical sites?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966570356/la-senia-alcanar-ermita-del-remei-castillo-de-ulldecona-return-through-the-montsia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sènia - Alcanar - Ermita del Remei - Castillo de Ulldecona – return through the Montsià&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, passes by historical sites such as the Ermita del Remei and the Castillo de Ulldecona, offering a cultural dimension to your ride.
tips:
The ascent to Puerto de Fredes is beautiful and of great cycling interest.
Traveling along the CV-105 road, the route follows the riverbed of the Riu Verde passing through the Ulldecona Reservoir, all with impressive views. Regarding the technical part, in the area prior to the tunnels, a 9% slope is reached.
Once overcome, it will be flat until the next section with a steep slope reaching 11% in the area of the horseshoe.
It ends with a section of about 8.5 km, very showy with several horseshoes with average slopes of 8-9%.
As its name suggests, this river charms with its emerald color. It is bordered by a path accessible to hikers and hikers, but the road already offers beautiful views of this waterway.

faqs.question: How challenging is the road cycling tour from Rosell to the port of Fredes?
faqs.answer: This road cycling tour is classified as difficult, requiring a very good physical condition. You can expect demanding climbs and descents with significant elevation changes, as the route navigates through mountainous and rugged terrain within the Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features wild, mountainous, and rugged terrain, typical of the Puertos de Beceite massif. The paths are generally paved with a firm surface, making them suitable for road bikes, but be prepared for sustained climbs and descents.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The tour is approximately 63 kilometers (39 miles) long with over 1200 meters (3900 feet) of elevation gain. It typically takes around 3 hours and 25 minutes to complete, but this can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience spectacular and well-preserved natural scenery. Key highlights include the challenging ascent to Puerto de Fredes and the picturesque stretch Along the riu Verd. The route also offers magnificent views of valleys like La Pobla and the broader Els Ports de la Sénia region, with lush forests and occasional waterfalls.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter while cycling in the Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà?
faqs.answer: The park is home to abundant wildlife. Keep an eye out for Iberian ibex, golden eagles, short-toed eagles, peregrine falcons, and griffon vultures, especially in the more remote mountainous sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this cycling tour in Rosell?
faqs.answer: The tour starts in Rosell. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Rosell is a small town, and you should be able to find suitable parking near the starting point, likely in municipal areas or along streets, before beginning your ride.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà?
faqs.answer: No, generally, you do not need special permits or to pay an entrance fee to cycle on public roads within the Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked routes, and be mindful of the natural environment.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' classification, substantial elevation gain, and demanding nature, this specific route is not recommended for families with young children or beginner road cyclists. It requires a high level of fitness and experience.

faqs.question: Is the Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà dog-friendly for cyclists?
faqs.answer: While the natural park generally allows dogs, cycling with a dog on this specific difficult road route might be challenging due to the distance, elevation, and traffic. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is well-trained, on a leash when appropriate, and that you have adequate water and provisions for it.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the scenery is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures and potentially adverse weather conditions in the mountainous areas.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route covers a significant portion of the official CIMA CS01 Fredes (46% of the route) and also includes sections of the Pedals dels Ports (22%). You will also briefly pass through parts of the Camí del Ròssec and Camí del Ballestar al Mangraner.
